raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased an item through XXXX for {$120.00}. The item was described as being in good condition with no defects. When it arrived, the item had significant issues that were not disclosed in the listing and did not match the sellers description. Because the item was significantly not as described, I attempted to open a return directly through XXXX, but their platform rules blocked the return label and prevented me from sending the item back to the seller. 

With no remedy available on the platform, I opened a dispute with American Express on XX/XX/XXXX and provided photographic documentation showing the defect and explaining that the item received differed materially from what was advertised. Instead of addressing any of the evidence I submitted, American Express denied the dispute and rebilled the charge on XX/XX/XXXX. Their written denial stated only that the merchandise has been delivered and cited the merchants all sales are final policywithout evaluating the evidence or the condition of the item. 

American Express did not follow the requirements of Regulation Z for resolving a billing error dispute. They did not consider photographic proof of the defect, the discrepancy between the listing and the item delivered, nor the platform barrier that prevented a return. Their decision relied exclusively on merchant policy rather than the merits of the claim or the condition of the goods. 

I am requesting CFPB assistance because American Express closed the dispute without conducting a reasonable investigation and billed me for merchandise that was not as described.
